# MusicTUI

> MusicTUI ("music") is an interactive TUI and CLI for Apple Music on macOS, plus a Claude Code plugin for natural-language control. It is free, open source (MIT), and written in Swift. Repository: https://github.com/anthonymaley/musictui

## What it does

- Interactive terminal UI (`music`) with four tabs: Now (album art, Up Next queue, playback controls), Playlists (browse, filter, pin, play), Speakers (AirPlay multi-room with per-speaker volume), Library (browse Artists/Albums/Songs with real cover art).
- Verified multi-room AirPlay: playback routes are checked against live network sessions (not AppleScript's claims) and self-heal if they fail to establish.
- Real album cover art rendered in the terminal (chafa truecolor half-blocks), fetched once and cached.
- Venue equalizer: eight presets (Nightclub, Dungeon, Open Air, Concert Hall, Jazz Club, Stadium, Cathedral, Late Night) created as genuine Music.app EQ presets.
- Catalog + library search with numbered, replayable results; playlist create/edit/share (iMessage, email); discovery (similar tracks, new releases, mixes, heavy rotation).
- Natural-language control from Claude Code via the /music skill: "Play Kid A in the kitchen and living room at 60%."
- Now-playing status line for the Claude Code prompt.

## Requirements

- macOS with the Music app and an Apple Music subscription.
- Swift 5.9+ (ships with Xcode) to build the CLI — one command, no Apple Developer account.
- Optional: an Apple Developer account ($99/yr) unlocks catalog/library search, playlist CRUD, and discovery via the Apple Music API. Playback, AirPlay routing, volume, and the TUI all work without it.

## Compared to other Apple Music terminal projects

Most "apple music tui/cli" projects are thin AppleScript wrappers (play/pause/skip) or require the third-party Cider player as a backend. MusicTUI drives the native Music.app directly, adds network-verified AirPlay routing, real cover art, a library browser, an EQ, and an LLM-native control surface, with a 250+ test suite.

## Links

- Site: https://musictui.com/
- Source + install: https://github.com/anthonymaley/musictui
- License: MIT
